{{Quote|I am Horak-mul, right hand of Ludo Kressh, the true Dark Lord of the Sith.|Horak-mul's spirit|<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
'''Horak-mul''' was a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. A member of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]], Horak-mul was native to an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. After the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]], Horak-mul sided with one of the contenders for the title, [[Ludo Kressh]]. Kressh's main rival was [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], a progressive [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Lord with expansionist ambitions. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], Sadow staged an attack on the Sith Empire by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and used this opportunity to declare himself the new Dark Lord of the Sith. This led to a schism between Sadow and Kressh, with Horak-mul siding with the latter. Sadow then staged another raid, this time leaving evidence that he was actually complicit; upon seeing this evidence, an angered Kressh ordered Horak-mul and a fellow supporter, [[Dor Gal-ram]], to gather their forces to attack Sadow. What ensued was a [[Battle of Khar Delba|skirmish]] at [[Khar Delba]], and Horak-mul and his allies were outwitted and defeated by Sadow. Horak-mul, along with Gal-ram, was killed in the battle when Sadow ordered the [[Sadow'een|crew]] of Horak-mul's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] to betray and kill him.

==Biography==
{{Quote|You summoned us, Lord Kressh. This had better be good.|Horak-mul|<ref name="pawns" />}}
Horak-mul was a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] and was native to a region in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] known as [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Space]].<ref name="flight" /> During his lifetime, Horak-mul participated in an event that was later known as the [[Subjugation of the False Apprentice]], and he created a number of [[Dor Gal-ram and Horak-mul's tablets|tablets]] alongside fellow [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Dor Gal-ram]].<ref name="Horak-Mul">{{TORcite|type=message|Horak-Mul|Engineer Sorrel}}</ref> In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], longtime [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]] [[Death/Legends|died]] after a century of iron-handed rule. Two [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Lords, [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Ludo Kressh]], stepped in to try and fill the power vacuum.<ref name="funeral">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]]</ref> Horak-mul supported Kressh, the more conservative Sith Lord, over Sadow, an ambitious Lord who wanted to expand the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire's]] boundaries.<ref name="pawns" /> During the [[Funeral of Marka Ragnos]], a [[Starship/Legends|ship]] coming from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]'', landed in [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban's]] [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], a canyon where the funeral was taking place, and its crew—the two [[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]]—were detained. A great debate began regarding the Humans' fate; Kressh wanted to execute them immediately, while Sadow wanted to keep them alive, believing that they had important information.<ref name="funeral" />

Finally, Sadow staged a Republic attack on the Sith Empire, riling the Sith into a frenzy. Following this attack, during a meeting of the [[Sith Council]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], Sadow declared himself the new Dark Lord of the Sith. Horak-mul watched on in silence as the majority of other Sith Lords voiced their support of Sadow. An outraged Kressh called his remaining supporters to rally around him, and Horak-mul left the meeting with the Sith Lord.<ref name="fabric">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]]</ref> After the meeting, Naga Sadow, now having been officially crowned the Dark Lord of the Sith, staged a Republic attack on the ''Starbreaker 12''{{'s}} hangar on Ziost and made off with the ship. Upon witnessing the scene, Ludo Kressh found Sadow's emblem amidst the carnage. Shocked and angry, Kressh summoned Horak-mul and Dor Gal-ram to his private world of [[Rhelg/Legends|Rhelg]] to confer on these developments. There, Kressh informed them of Sadow's complicity in the assault and ordered Horak-mul and Gal-ram to gather their [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] to stage a surprise attack on Sadow.<ref name="pawns" />

Horak-mul did as he was commanded, massing his forces and joining Kressh's and Gal-ram's fleets at [[Khar Delba]], the site of [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|Sadow's citadel]].<ref name="pawns" /> They commenced their [[Battle of Khar Delba|attack]], launching a rain of fire on Sadow's base. Horak-mul exclaimed that victory was inevitable; however, Sadow had another trick up his sleeve. Sadow had hidden his fleet in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around Khar Delba's [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Khar Shian]], and the fleet attacked and caught Horak-mul and his allies off-guard. Sadow then moved on to the next stage of his plan, ordering the crew of Horak-mul's ship,<ref name="flight" /> in reality [[Sadow'een]]—Sadow's elite assassins<ref name="Bitter Rivalries">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|Bitter Rivalries|Hoth}}</ref>—to kill him. The [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] crew attacked Horak-mul while he was distracted by the battle, caught him unaware, and murdered him<ref name="flight" />—though Horak-mul would later claim that it took five assassins to kill him.<ref name="Bitter Rivalries" /> Dor Gal-ram was killed by the same treachery, and Kressh was defeated in the Battle of Khar Delba.<ref name="flight" />

The artificial [[eye of Horak-mul]] was eventually lost, but was retrieved in [[3963 BBY]], over a thousand years after Horak-mul's death, by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Covenant Shadow]] [[Celeste Morne]].<ref name="vector" /> Horak-mul was later buried on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] by his followers, though scavengers removed [[Horak-mul's sarcophagus]] from the moon over a thousand years later and were carrying it aboard their ship, the [[Starrunner (light freighter)|''Starrunner'']], when the Sith Lord's [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] drove the crew insane above the planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. A few years later, in [[3642 BBY]],<ref name="Time">{{TOR dates|2}}</ref> Horak-mul's spirit was possessing the body of an [[Ortolan/Legends|Ortolan]] chief when he encountered the [[Sith Inquisitor]] [[Darth Nox|Kallig]] of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]]. Kallig possessed the power of [[Force walk]], the ability to bind Force ghosts to one's own body and draw upon their power, and Horak-mul agreed to allow Kallig to bind him if the Sith would perform a task for him: the destruction of a [[Sadow'een Temple]] on Hoth.<ref name="Bitter Rivalries" />

[[File:Horak-Mul.png|thumb|left|160px|Horak-mul's ghost]]
After Kallig desecrated the temple, Horak-mul possessed the shell of a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] in order to inform Kallig of the ''Starrunner''{{'s}} location. Upon locating Horak-mul's spirit in the depths of the crashed [[superdreadnaught]] ''[[Star of Coruscant]]'', Kallig bound Horak-mul's spirit,<ref name="Dreadnought">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|The Fallen Dreadnought|Hoth}}</ref> and the ghost aided the Inquisitor in the young Sith's battle against the Sith Lord [[Darth Thanaton]].<ref name="Conflagration">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|Conflagration|Dromund Kaas}}</ref> However, Horak-mul and the other Sith spirits whom Kallig had bound later mentally harassed Kallig, threatening to drive the Sith insane. As he was haunting the Inquisitor's thoughts, Horak-mul said: "You think you're the puppeteer, but you're just the puppet.<ref name="Visions">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|Visions and Visionaries|Voss}}</ref>

Eventually, Kallig discovered a mind-healing ritual on [[Voss]] that could the subdue the ghosts. With help from a [[Voss (species)|Voss]] dream-walking cult, Kallig confronted Horak-mul in a dream. The Sith Lord assumed the appearance of Darth Thanaton but his illusion was destroyed when a [[Gormak/Legends|Gormak]] Force-shaman named [[Hadrik]] used a dream-rock to force Horak-mul to reveal himself.<ref name="Ritual">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|The Ritual|Voss}}</ref> Kallig then used Horak-mul's power, along with the other ghosts, to finally defeat Thanaton and assume his seat on the [[Dark Council]]. Though the other ghosts pleaded to be freed, Horak-mul willingly decided to stay, knowing that he'd at least see [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] rather than be bound to his tomb on Hoth.<ref name="Dark Council">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|The Dark Council|Korriban}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|We have been careful to hide our own doubts about Sadow's foolish dreams of conquest.|Horak-mul|<ref name="pawns" />}}
Horak-mul was a staunch supporter of Ludo Kressh and considered Naga Sadow's ambitions to expand the Sith Empire as self-destructive and foolish. However, due to the popularity among Sith Lords of supporting Sadow, and the risks inherent in speaking out against the Dark Lord of the Sith, Horak-mul was careful to hide his opposition to Sadow's dreams of conquest. Before the attack on the ''Starbreaker 12'' that Sadow manipulated to appear as a Republic raid, Horak-mul considered the Republic a myth; however, the attack convinced him otherwise. Horak-mul sported an artificial right eye<ref name="pawns" /> known as the Eye of Horak-mul. He also wore a [[Horak-mul's ceremonial robe|ceremonial robe]].<ref name="Horak-Mul" /> During his duel with Kallig on Voss, the spirit of Horak-mul wielded a purple-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="Ritual" />

==Behind the scenes==
Horak-mul first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series, which was written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1996]]. In these appearances, Horak-mul was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] He was later mentioned in [[Knights of the Old Republic 25|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 25]]. In [[2008]], Horak-mul received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.

If the Sith Inquisitor chooses to free the ghosts at the end of Act III of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', Horak-mul refuses to depart and stays on with the Inquisitor. However, if the Inquisitor was more light sided, they would have the option to let the ghosts pass on.<ref name="Dark Council" />
